skill.name: neuropixels-analysis
skill.description: End-to-end Neuropixels extracellular electrophysiology analysis pipeline using SpikeInterface. Supports preprocessing, motion correction, Kilosort4 spike sorting, QC metrics, and Allen/IBL-style curation for Neuropixels recordings.
skill.summary: Complete pipeline for Neuropixels electrophysiology data from raw recording to curated units.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Evaluated 692 static findings across 19 files (5520 lines). All findings are FALSE POSITIVES: 545 'shell backtick execution' alerts are markdown code blocks in documentation files, not executable code. 70+ 'weak cryptographic algorithm' and 'system reconnaissance' alerts are neuroscience terminology (SNR, detect, explore, control) misinterpreted by the scanner. Filesystem operations write analysis results to user-specified output directories. Network URLs are documentation citations. The heuristic 'DANGEROUS COMBINATION' finding aggregates these false positives. The previous audit (neuropixels-analysis_audit_result_v1.json) passed all gates including security. No confirmed malicious patterns found.

low_findings.title: Shell command patterns in documentation files only
low_findings.description: 545 static findings flagged 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' across all reference .md files. All instances are Python or shell code examples inside markdown fenced code blocks showing legitimate SpikeInterface API usage. These are documentation, not executable code. FALSE POSITIVE - no command injection or execution risk.

low_findings.title: Filesystem writes to user-specified output directories
low_findings.description: 7 filesystem write operations detected. All write to user-specified output directories (JSON results, CSV metrics, PNG plots). This is expected and required behavior for a data analysis pipeline. Files are written to explicit output paths passed as arguments, not to system directories. FALSE POSITIVE as security concern.

low_findings.title: Documentation URLs flagged as network activity
low_findings.description: 17 network URL findings pointing to documentation and reference URLs (spikeinterface.readthedocs.io, github.com, docs.anthropic.com). All are citation links in comments and markdown documentation. No actual network requests are made by the skill's Python scripts. FALSE POSITIVE.

low_findings.title: Heuristic combination false positive
low_findings.description: Critical heuristic 'DANGEROUS COMBINATION: Code execution + Network + Credential access' aggregates individual false positive findings. No actual credential access or code execution exists. The individual factors (documentation code blocks, citation URLs, placeholder API keys in docs) are all benign. FALSE POSITIVE.

low_findings.title: Placeholder API keys in documentation examples
low_findings.description: 2 env_access findings for 'Generic API/secret keys' at AI_CURATION.md lines 298 and 307. These are placeholder strings 'your-api-key' in documentation code examples showing how to use AI curation clients. No actual API keys are hardcoded in any Python script. The actual skill scripts do not use environment variables or API keys.

content.value_statement: Neuropixels recordings produce massive high-density extracellular data that is difficult to process manually. This skill automates the full pipeline from raw SpikeGLX/Open Ephys/NWB files to curated spike-sorted units using proven Allen Institute and IBL protocols.
actual_capabilities: Load recordings from SpikeGLX, Open Ephys, and NWB formats into a unified SpikeInterface pipeline
Apply preprocessing chain: bandpass filtering, phase shift correction, bad channel detection, and common average referencing
Estimate and correct probe drift or tissue motion with rigid and non-rigid correction presets
Run Kilosort4 spike sorting with GPU acceleration and alternative CPU sorters
Compute quality metrics including SNR, ISI violations, presence ratio, and amplitude cutoff for unit evaluation
Curate units using Allen/IBL-style criteria and export results to Phy and NWB formats
limitations: Kilosort4 requires a CUDA-capable GPU; CPU-only environments must use alternative sorters like SpykingCircus2 or Mountainsort5
Pipeline performance depends on recording size and available system memory for large Neuropixels datasets
Curation thresholds are standardized presets (Allen, IBL, strict) and may need tuning for specific experimental conditions
AI-assisted visual curation requires an external API client and is optional; it is not part of the core pipeline

use_cases.title: Batch process multiple Neuropixels recordings
use_cases.description: Automate preprocessing, spike sorting, and quality control for batches of Neuropixels recordings from chronic probes or multiple animals.
use_cases.target_user: Neuroscience researcher processing large-scale electrophysiology datasets

use_cases.title: Standardize lab curation workflow
use_cases.description: Apply consistent Allen or IBL curation criteria across all recordings in a lab to ensure reproducible unit classification for publications.
use_cases.target_user: Lab manager or PI standardizing data analysis protocols

use_cases.title: Quick exploratory analysis of new recordings
use_cases.description: Load and inspect a new Neuropixels recording to check data quality, detect bad channels, assess drift, and preview signal characteristics before full processing.
use_cases.target_user: Graduate student beginning analysis of new experimental data

prompt_templates.title: Full pipeline from raw data
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the complete Neuropixels analysis pipeline on my SpikeGLX recording at /path/to/data. Use Kilosort4 for spike sorting, apply motion correction, and curate units with Allen Institute criteria. Save all outputs to /path/to/output.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has raw Neuropixels data and wants end-to-end processing

prompt_templates.title: Preprocessing only
prompt_templates.prompt: Preprocess my Neuropixels recording from /path/to/data. Apply a 300 Hz high-pass filter, detect and remove bad channels, apply phase shift correction, and use common median reference. Save the preprocessed recording to /path/to/output.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to preprocess data before running a custom sorter

prompt_templates.title: Drift assessment and correction
prompt_templates.prompt: Check my Neuropixels recording at /path/to/data for probe drift. Estimate motion using the kilosort_like preset and visualize the drift map. If drift exceeds 10 micrometers, apply nonrigid motion correction.
prompt_templates.scenario: User suspects probe drift and wants to evaluate and fix it

prompt_templates.title: Custom curation with specific thresholds
prompt_templates.prompt: Compute quality metrics for my sorted units and apply strict curation: SNR greater than 5, ISI violations ratio less than 0.01, presence ratio greater than 0.95, and amplitude cutoff less than 0.05. Label units that do not pass as MUA.
prompt_templates.scenario: Experienced user wants to apply custom curation thresholds

output_examples.input: Process a SpikeGLX recording from a Neuropixels 1.0 probe through the full pipeline.
output: Preprocessed recording with bad channels removed and phase shift corrected
Drift map visualization showing peak activity across time and depth
Kilosort4 sorting results with unit IDs, spike times, and templates
Quality metrics table with SNR, ISI violations, presence ratio per unit
Curated unit labels and Phy-compatible export for manual review

output_examples.input: Check my preprocessed recording for motion drift before sorting.
output: Detected peaks plotted on a drift map showing activity across recording time
Estimated drift range in micrometers (e.g., estimated drift range: 15.3 um)
Recommendation on whether motion correction is needed based on drift threshold

best_practices: Always inspect drift maps before spike sorting - even moderate drift above 10 um can reduce sorting quality significantly
Document curation thresholds in publications and adjust them based on recording quality rather than using defaults without verification
Run the pipeline on a short test segment first to validate parameters before processing full-length recordings
anti_patterns: Skipping bad channel detection or phase shift correction for Neuropixels 1.0 data, which introduces systematic noise and alignment artifacts
Applying motion correction by default without verifying drift exists first, which can introduce artifacts in stable recordings
Using strict curation thresholds on noisy recordings, which may discard valid units; always match criteria to data quality

faq.question: What file formats does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports SpikeGLX (.ap.bin/.lf.bin/.meta), Open Ephys (.continuous/.oebin), and NWB (.nwb) formats through SpikeInterface readers.

faq.question: Do I need a GPU to run Kilosort4?
faq.answer: Yes, Kilosort4 requires a CUDA-capable GPU. CPU alternatives include SpykingCircus2, Mountainsort5, and Tridesclous2.

faq.question: What curation methods are available?
faq.answer: Three methods: Allen Institute (permissive, good for exploration), IBL (stricter, for publications), and strict (conservative single-unit criteria).

faq.question: Can this skill handle multi-probe recordings?
faq.answer: Yes, use the stream_id parameter to select specific probe streams from multi-probe SpikeGLX or Open Ephys recordings.

faq.question: Where are the analysis outputs saved?
faq.answer: All outputs go to the user-specified output directory. This includes preprocessed data, sorting results, quality metrics CSV, curation labels JSON, and Phy/NWB exports.

faq.question: Does this skill use AI for curation?
faq.answer: The core pipeline uses automated threshold-based curation (Allen/IBL). Optional AI-assisted visual curation requires an external API client and is documented separately.
